     The Numbers Business Report

Bewitched (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $63,313,159Details
International Box Office $67,846,147
Worldwide Box Office $131,159,306
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Movie star Jack Wyatt has hit a rough patch in his career. Looking to make a comeback, he signs on to play the role of Darrin in a movie version of the popular 1960s sitcom “Bewitched.” Jack insists that an unknown is hired to play Darrin’s better half, the beguiling witch-housewife Samantha, so the studio finds an up-and-comer named Isabel Bigelow. Once shooting begins, though, Jack learns that Isabel was cast too well — since she actually is a witch.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$20,131,130 (31.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.15 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:48.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$80,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,174 opening theaters/3,188 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $111,711,664

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 24th, 2005 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: July 8th, 2005 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: October 25th, 2005 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some language, including sex and drug references, and partial nudity.
Running Time: 98 minutes
Plot point: Aspiring Actress / Actor, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance, Secret Magic, Witches
Social setting: TV Industry
Subgenre: Farcical / Slapstick Comedy
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Movie star Jack Wyatt has hit a rough patch in his career. Looking to make a comeback, he signs on to play the role of Darrin in a movie version of the popular 1960s sitcom “Bewitched.” Jack insists that an unknown is hired to play Darrin’s better half, the beguiling witch-housewife Samantha, so the studio finds an up-and-comer named Isabel Bigelow. Once shooting begins, though, Jack learns that Isabel was cast too well — since she actually is a witch.
